prose:
The fukui-reactivity task on acrolein was performed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier `standard`). The calculation was run in the gas phase with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1 for the neutral parent molecule. The geometry was taken from the provided `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz` file. This calculation was used to derive the Fukui functions from single-point energy calculations of the neutral, cation (+1, multiplicity 2), and anion (-1, multiplicity 2) states, performed using PySCF. Density fitting was not used. The integrity checks for charge conservation were deemed acceptable, with the sum of f+ and f- being approximately 1.0.

The Fukui indices and dual descriptor values for each atom are as follows:

| Atom | Fukui f+ (electrophilic) | Fukui f- (nucleophilic) | Fukui f0 (radical) | Dual Descriptor (DD) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| O (atom 1) | 0.40136 | 0.39389 | 0.39763 | 0.00748 |
| C (atom 2) | 0.02226 | 0.00599 | 0.01412 | 0.01627 |
| C (atom 3) | 0.43405 | 0.09140 | 0.26272 | 0.34264 |
| C (atom 4) | 0.64232 | 0.08737 | 0.36485 | 0.55495 |
| H (atom 5) | -0.15726 | 0.08596 | -0.03565 | -0.24322 |
| H (atom 6) | -0.20720 | 0.18568 | -0.01076 | -0.39288 |
| H (atom 7) | -0.06212 | 0.08449 | 0.01118 | -0.14660 |
| H (atom 8) | -0.07341 | 0.06523 | -0.00409 | -0.13863 |

The most electrophilic site (highest f+) is C (atom 4) with an f+ value of 0.64232.
The most nucleophilic site (highest f-) is O (atom 1) with an f- value of 0.39389.
The calculation passed all integrity checks and is considered trustworthy.
